31/45x18 = 12 yrs. 4m. 24d, A better method of calculating
this would be from the longitude of the Moon. You know her
longitude to be lOMO'-Sl '-45*. This also gives the second
Pada of Satabhisak. You remember also that a whole Naksatra
covers l3o-20'. This is equal to 800 minutes. So for one Pada
of a star the number of minutes required is 200. Now the
second Pada of this star will be complete at 13°-20'-0". So the
balance in the second Pada is 13o-20'-0* minus 10'-51'-45* =
20-2S'-lS'. Now what is the Dasa for one Pada of this star ?
It is 4i yrs. Now convert 2:'-28'-15' into minutes. You get
148.25. You know that it is 9/2 yrs. for 200 minutes, then how
many years etc. you get for 14825 minutes ? That comes to
5.93x9/16=3 yrs. 4 m. Id. This is the balance of Dasa in
the second Pada of that star. There are two full Padas or one
full half yet to be covered. For that the Dasa-period is 9 yrs.
Add this to 3y-4m-ld. You get then 12 yrs. 4m. l,d. as the
